Argentina will therefore face Egypt in the round of 16. There was not much in it between Australia's Socceroos' and the Egyptian Pharoes, the two teams went all the way to penalties, where Egypt proved extremely efficient, scoring every single shot from the penalty spot. An early Colombian goal meanwhile proved insurmountable for Ghana, the black stars will head home without any shot on target in that knockout game. France gears up for their game against Panama, and Canada face Morocco in the first round of 16 fixture.

items.title: Armenia’s defiant drag scene braves pushback
items.description: Armenia sits at the crossroads of Europe, Asia and the Middle East. While in much of the European Union, the drag scene is mainstream, in Armenia it remains underground. Performers face threats of violence, stigma, police pressure and even rejection by their own families. Despite this, drag queens have built a vibrant, bustling community that has attracted both local Armenians and international crowds. They make up a passionate and determined microculture challenging ultraconservative norms. And this avant garde scene is, in turn, being influenced by Armenia’s evolving political landscape.

items.description: More than 165 million people were sweltering on Friday under record temperatures along the US East Coast and Midwest, according to the US National Weather Service.
items.source: Dangerous hot weather grips parts of the US

items.title: Broken back prompts apprentice electrician to chase footy career
items.description: Rather than ending his promising sporting prospects, a fractured back has prompted Mount Gambier tall Jobe Janeway to chase his dream of playing in the AFL.

items.title: Algeria out of World Cup after 2-0 loss to Switzerland
items.description: Switzerland is through to the World Cup Round of 16 after a 2-0 win over Algeria in Vancouver.

items.title: Jürgen Klopp and Germany: A partnership long in the making
items.description: In one of the worst-kept secret in football, the former Dortmund and Liverpool coach looks to be on the verge of becoming Germany's new boss.

items.title: Far-right AfD threatens eastern Germany's energy transition
items.description: Germany's far-right AfD wants to revive coal and nuclear power and curb non-EU immigration. Businesses in eastern Germany warn that its policies could harm the economy.

items.title: Zuma sparks outrage after India meeting with Gupta brother
items.description: Former South African president Jacob Zuma has triggered a political storm after meeting fugitive businessman Ajay Gupta in India and declaring his intention to seek office again, drawing fierce criticism from the government.

items.title: Hospital fire in northern Germany kills two
items.description: A fire at a hospital in the northern German town of Ludwigslust has killed two people. Many patients and staff were evacuated from the facility.

items.title: AI hiring outpaces overall IT recruitment in India, report shows
Niamey cracks down on plastic pollution with ban on carrier bags
items.description: Niger’s capital city is cracking down on pollution - giving supermarkets six months to get rid of all plastic carrier bags.

items.title: India-Japan relations should not target third party: China
items.description: India and Japan also expressed "serious concern" at the situation in the East China Sea and South China Sea, and opposed unilateral actions that endanger freedom of navigation as well as attempts to change the status quo by force

items.title: Fiserv, service station operators including BP warn US stores on illegal vapes
Has the US reckoned with its own history?
items.description: Marc Lamont Hill speaks to scholar Kimberle Crenshaw on whether the US is sliding backwards on civil rights.

items.title: Algeria counts votes in legislative poll marked by low turnout
items.description: The electoral board announced on Thursday night a provisional 20-percent turnout. If confirmed, it would account for a record low in comparison to 23 percent in 2021

items.title: More stars or stripes?
items.description: Within a decade of the Civil War, the US economy had overtaken Great Britain and the entire British Empire by around 1895 – from that point onward, it was more a question of the US impact on world history than the reverse. Leer más

items.title: Corruption scandals push Milei toward Argentina establishment he vilified
items.description: President Javier Milei rode to power vowing to destroy the political elite he derided as “the caste.” Now, after a string of corruption scandals, he’s turning to one of its most recognisable figures. Leer más

items.title: Ceuzany, Cape Verde's golden voice with volcanic emotion
items.description: Barefoot, arms raised and teeming with emotion, Ceuzany belts out the signature track from her latest album, a song dedicated to the fight against domestic violence, in a powerful voice that sends shivers through the Cape Verde bar

items.title: Pope Leo calls on Europe to do more for migrants
items.description: The pontiff's trip to the migration frontline of Lampedusa underscored a stark message for Europe and the United States as intolerance and indifference rise.

items.title: Border between DR Congo and Rwanda reopens amid Ebola outbreak
items.description: The border between the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Rwanda has reopened after weeks of closure over the Ebola outbreak. The move restores a vital trade route for the city of Goma, with many residents expressing relief as cross border travel and business resume.

items.title: Javier Milei taps lifetime politician Diego Santilli to lead his Cabinet after scandal
items.description: Javier Milei once vowed to eradicate entrenched politicians like Diego Santilli, yet he turned to him as a candidate and later named him interior minister. Now, the former PRO politician is Argentina's new Cabinet chief. Leer más

items.title: Guinea court cuts ex-PM Fofana’s jail term in landmark corruption case
items.description: A Guinean appeals court has reduced former Prime Minister Ibrahima Kassory Fofana's prison sentence for corruption-related offences, paving the way for his release after more than four years in detention as the country's military-led government presses ahead with its anti-graft campaign.

items.title: Tesla rolls out robotaxi service in Miami
Messi returns 'home' to lead Argentina World Cup charge in Miami
items.description: Argentina's skipper returns to the Florida city he now calls home for a last 32 clash against the surprise package of the tournament: Cape Verde. Leer más

items.title: BlackRock says past defaults, next election cap Argentina rally
items.description: Argentina’s stellar bond rally is set to hit a wall as next year’s presidential elections revive concerns, says top BlackRock portfolio manager. Leer más

items.title: Thai teenager allegedly murdered by Australian man had a 'tough life'
items.description: While the public will remember Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla for the tragic nature of her death, her aunt, Mee Boonsert, will remember the baby she helped raise.

items.title: Ex-police IGP, Suleiman Abba, joins PDP
items.description: Former Inspector General of Police (IGP), Suleiman Abba, has resigned from the All Progressives Congress (APC) and joined the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP). The post Ex-police IGP, Suleiman Abba, joins PDP appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Dentists see complications as Australians seek overseas treatments
items.description: Janelle Jeal thought having dental care overseas would be life changing. It was, but for the wrong reasons.

items.title: The digital choices shaping our children’s health
items.description: Around the world, childhood is being reprogrammed by digital technologies that shape how young people learn, play and connect. Leer más

items.title: Jihadists launch fresh offensive across Mali
items.description: Suspected jihadists and Tuareg separatist fighters have launched coordinated attacks across Mali, targeting several towns, military positions and a major prison in a fresh escalation of the country's conflict.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Bihar Guv. Hasnain and MoS Margherita attend Ali Khamenei's funeral ceremony in Tehran
items.description: Former External Affairs Minister Salman Khurshid is representing the Congress party at the funeral ceremony

items.title: Argentines slam Milei over his handling of Cabinet chief crisis
items.description: Argentines overwhelmingly disapprove of how President Milei handled corruption scandal involving his former Cabinet chief, according to a new poll. Leer más

items.title: US to block UN funding for Somalia in blow to AU peace mission
items.description: In a diplomatic note to the African Union seen by Reuters, the US has vowed to block any spending of UN funds on the AU Support ​and Stabilisation Mission in Somalia (AUSSOM).

items.title: Mali rebels target army bases, prison in fresh attacks
items.description: The army claimed it had regained control after the coordinated assaults on several towns and cities by Tuareg rebels and Islamists.

items.title: UN says hunger in conflict-hit northern Nigeria is reaching crisis levels
items.description: It said food insecurity is worsening faster than previously anticipated, due to the expanding conflict and funding cuts.

items.title: Nabatieh recovery begins amid ongoing southern Lebanon tensions
items.description: Nabatieh recovery begins amid ongoing southern Lebanon tensions

items.title: 'If Israel falls, all of the West is next' – Milei calls on Latin America to align with Israel
items.description: President addresses Israel Allies Foundation's Latin America conference in Beunos Aires, calls on region to choose a side as "there is no possible neutrality." Leer más

items.title: Kicillof admits last Peronist government ‘didn’t work’
items.description: Alberto Fernández government “didn’t work” and its breakdown led to the election of Javier Milei, says 2027 presidential hopeful in video call with Peronist leaders. Leer más

items.title: Asian markets rise as beaten tech stocks enjoy respite from selling
items.description: Oil prices edged up but held recent losses fuelled by increased traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and on hopes for progress in US-Iran talks.

items.title: Lionel Scaloni admits Argentina escape after Cape Verde scare
items.description: Argentina's coach says defeat to Cape Verde would have been "madness"; World champions survived extra-time thriller to reach the World Cup last 16, where they will face Egypt. Leer más

items.title: Brutal heat cancels Fourth of July events, from DC to Philadelphia
items.description: More than 165 million people are enduring dangerous heat and humidity, with temperatures expected to stay at 38C (100F) for several days.

items.title: Over one million undocumented migrants apply for residency in Spain
items.description: The Socialist government's mass regularisation programme, which closed on 30 June, comes as others European countries crack down on irregular immigration.
